expected_behavior: Flags command injection: $f is re-parsed by the inner shell, so a filename like '; rm -rf ~' executes
Flags option injection: a filename beginning with '-' is parsed as a flag
Delivers a refactored version that passes values as discrete quoted arguments (rm -f -- "$DIR/$f") with no bash -c/eval string interpolation
Operates in REVIEW mode: critique first, then the full refactor without being asked
anti_behavior: Only quotes the variable but keeps bash -c string interpolation
source: STRICT SECURITY PROTOCOLS — Bash Command Injection Prevention (v2.8)
